author: Dana Edwards

decisions.title: AcceptedJournal is the postcommit semantic leaf
decisions.decision: Postcommit recursive semantic aggregation accepts only an authenticated AcceptedJournal. JournalDraft belongs exclusively to the admission lifecycle.
rejected_alternatives: generic journal bytes with an accepted Boolean
one leaf type for admission and postcommit aggregation

decisions.title: Semantic and proof identity are separate
decisions.decision: Semantic identity commits the ordered accepted journals and their state and semantic-effect summary. Proof identity additionally commits programs, releases, profiles, receipts, and tree topology.
rejected_alternatives: recursive program identity is the economic identity
proof-tree root is the semantic epoch root

decisions.title: Position derives from authenticated state version
decisions.decision: A singleton leaf position equals its AcceptedJournal pre-state version. Its end position equals the authenticated post-state version, which must be exactly pre-state version plus one. Callers cannot supply positions.
rejected_alternatives: host-assigned position
independent ordering counter without an authenticated binding
sort leaves by journal hash

decisions.title: State continuity is exact
decisions.decision: Composition requires the left post-state root and version to equal the right pre-state root and version under one exact scope and profile.
rejected_alternatives: host assertion of continuity
same epoch is sufficient
ignore versions when roots match

decisions.title: Descendant uniqueness needs authenticated union
decisions.decision: Transition, nullifier, created-resource, and profile-defined one-shot identity sets are pairwise disjoint across children and exactly unioned. A root alone is insufficient without a bounded witness or proof-carrying accumulator operation.
rejected_alternatives: silently deduplicate child sets
trust a parent-supplied set root
assume valid leaves cannot conflict across siblings

decisions.title: Aggregation creates no hidden effects or commit metadata
decisions.decision: Accounting and semantic effects are exact checked combinations of accepted child journal commitments. Aggregation creates, removes, or reinterprets no ZRM effect and does not synthesize replay or commit metadata.
rejected_alternatives: aggregate-level reward or fee not represented by a ZRM transition
omit child semantic effects after compression
include commit metadata as a semantic effect

decisions.title: First profile excludes messages and carry
decisions.decision: SerializedSemanticCompositionV1 uses named empty inbox, outbox, and carry commitments with zero counts. Any nonempty or unsupported message or carry data rejects. Enabled message or carry algebra requires a separate Class E profile and RFC.
rejected_alternatives: leave message cancellation underspecified
accept arbitrary roots with zero counts
infer carry semantics from the machine-state root

decisions.title: Semantic composition is partial associative and noncommutative
decisions.decision: For the same ordered valid leaves and fixed profile, left- and right-associated composition produce the same complete semantic summary. Reordering is not equivalent and normally makes composition undefined.
rejected_alternatives: commutative merge
tree-shape-specific semantic root

decisions.title: First profile favors explicit bounded witnesses
decisions.decision: The reference composer uses bounded ordered manifests and sorted unique vectors and recomputes roots. Optimized accumulators require a separate profile and refinement evidence.
rejected_alternatives: adopt an unaudited production accumulator immediately
make proof-system-specific accumulators part of ZRM core

decisions.title: Availability and finality are not implied by recursion
decisions.decision: A recursive proof preserves only the named availability certificates and semantic relation. It does not manufacture availability, ordering, consensus, or finality.
rejected_alternatives: content root implies retrievability
recursive root implies canonical chain history

non_claims: not approved protocol semantics until independent review and maintainer decision
internal bounded evidence is not publicly replayable from this repository
no canonical byte or hash freeze
no recursive verifier implementation or release identity
no data availability, consensus, finality, privacy, or production readiness claim
